title: cerastium decalvans f. silvaticum
intro: Cerastium decalvans f. silvaticum, also known as the woodlander mouse-ear, is a subspecies of the Cerastium decalvans plant, a flowering plant in the carnation family. This plant has been used in traditional medicine to treat a range of ailments, including digestive issues and inflammation, due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The woodlander mouse-ear is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, easing respiratory problems, and promoting wound healing. In herbal preparations, the plant is typically used in teas, infusions, and tinctures, although more research is needed to fully understand its medicinal properties and potential uses.
